title: rubus serpens subsp. angustifrons
intro: Rubus serpens subsp. angustifrons, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan dewberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rubus serpens subsp. angustifrons include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies for its potential antiviral and antibacterial properties.
